app.title: A4 Handwriting Workbook
app.subtitle: Intensive print-writing, patience, and concentration training with a website layout on screen and A4 layout only when printing.
app.screenHint: On screen, this document behaves like a website. When printing, CSS switches to A4 sheets with dedicated margins, breaks, and spacing.

sections.objective: Write legibly, without rushing, and review before handing in.
sections.coreRuleText: The student does not finish because the page is full. Work continues until the timer ends and the minimum quality criterion is met.
sections.coreSentence: The goal is not to finish. The goal is to keep quality until the timer rings.
sections.painWarningText: If there is pain, numbness, excessive trembling, real crying, or intense fatigue, stop handwriting and switch to reading, oral dictation, or a break.
sections.dailyRoutineText: Six structured hours, but not six hours of copying. Direct handwriting is split into short blocks with real breaks.
sections.diagnosisKicker: Keep it without over-correcting: this is the before sample

notes.cover: Use this cover to assemble the before/after portfolio.
notes.parent: The adult acts as a coach: routine, short feedback, and visual criteria.
notes.quality: Quality increases before speed. If handwriting gets worse, speed goes back down.
notes.review: The task ends after writing, reviewing, and correcting.
notes.monotony: The monotonous part is intentional: it trains continuing with quality even when it is boring.

goodReinforcement: You redid it without giving up.
beforeBlock: Head not glued to the paper
deliveryPt: Do my words have enough space?
Does the sentence start with a capital letter?
Does the sentence end with a period?
Did I review before saying I was done?
Did I rush or stay controlled?
deliveryDe: Beginnt der Satz mit einem Großbuchstaben?
Endet der Satz mit einem Punkt?
Habe ich kontrolliert, bevor ich fertig gesagt habe?
War ich zu schnell oder kontrolliert?

rows: compare with model and mark best letters

round.name: Round letters
round.focus: Close a, separate a/o, and distinguish d/q and g/q.

vertical.name: Vertical letters
vertical.focus: Straight lines, visible dots, and j below the line.

bridges.name: Bridge letters
bridges.focus: Consistent arches; n does not become u; m has three parts.

curves.name: Small curves
curves.focus: Open e, legible s, and z with clear diagonals.

diagonals.name: Stems and diagonals
diagonals.focus: b goes up, p goes down, v is not u, and w is not m.

uppercaseRound.name: Round capitals
uppercaseRound.focus: Closed O, open C, marked G, and D not turning into O.

numbersGerman.name: Numbers and German characters
numbersGerman.focus: Clear numbers, visible umlauts, and recognizable ß.

week1.title: control, line, pressure, and routine
week1.goal: Build posture, speed control, pencil pressure, and timer acceptance.
week1.metric: Complete 5-8 min repetitions without rushing and produce a before/after comparison.

week2.title: lowercase by motor families
week2.goal: Practice lowercase letters by movement groups.
week2.metric: More legible lowercase alphabet and fewer confusing pairs.

week3.title: capitals, numbers, and German characters
week3.metric: Complete alphabet, clear numbers, and visible umlauts.

week4.title: frequent German words and patience
week4.goal: Turn trained letters into functional words.
week4.metric: Legible lists with 18-20 min monotony blocks.

week5.title: school sentences, dictation, and review
week5.metric: Legible school sentences with mandatory review.

week6.title: endurance, controlled speed, and transfer
week6.goal: Write well even when tired or bored.
week6.metric: Before/after portfolio and controlled speed without destroying legibility.
